reasoning: Historically, the base rate for any country to win Eurovision is around 1/37 (about 3%), though Italy is typically a strong contender with solid historical performance. Before its national selection, Italy was listed among the early favorites in the betting odds. However, the winner of the 2025 Sanremo Music Festival, Olly, declined the opportunity to represent Italy, passing it to the runner-up, Lucio Corsi, with the song 'Volevo essere un duro'. Following this announcement in late February, Italy's standing in the betting odds plummeted, falling from 6th to around 15th place. Current Italian media reporting (as of March 1, 2025) explicitly states that bookmakers give Lucio Corsi a 2% chance of winning Eurovision 2025. This 2% bookmaker estimate perfectly corroborates the Polymarket trading price of 0.02 from February 20. Given the general efficiency of comprehensive Eurovision prediction markets and the sharp negative market reaction to the artist change, there is strong evidence supporting an estimate of exactly 2%.
